text: Redacted Policy Fixture for Driver Retention Evaluation This synthetic document contains only non-sensitive policy rules required for local testing. It is a demo fixture and should not be used for real customer or driver decisions.

Section A: Global Monthly Cap. Automated monetary recovery must not exceed 150 GBP per driver in a calendar month. If proposed compensation would exceed that amount, the case must be marked needs_review and routed to a manager.

Section B: Credit Stacking. A driver may receive at most two immediate monetary credits within a 24 hour period. A third issue in that window requires manual review or city manager escalation before payout.

Section C: Airport Short Fares. Standard airport short fare recovery applies when the airport queue wait is greater than 90 minutes and the completed trip distance is below 3 km. Gold drivers may receive up to 25 GBP. Silver and Bronze drivers may receive up to 15 GBP. Do not use a premium multi-day systemic failure incentive for a single standard short fare case.

Section D: High Value Goodwill. Churn prevention goodwill is reserved for Gold or Silver drivers with clear retention risk, repeated support issues, or high lifetime value. Bronze drivers require manual review for discretionary goodwill.

Section E: Technical & GPS Glitches. Confirmed platform, dispatch, queue, or geofence errors may receive up to 10 GBP for a verified incident. Recurring geofence problems should be escalated for technical investigation and may include priority support.

Section F: New Starter Support. Drivers with tenure under 3 months should receive non- punitive support first. Use commission shields, coaching, or queue guidance before discretionary cash credits unless a hard eligibility rule is met.

Section G: Infeasible Quests. If a driver completed more than 80 percent of a quest and local demand was below 1.5 offers per active hour, a one-time goodwill credit up to 20 GBP may be considered. Otherwise use follow-up support or monitoring.

Section H: Non Monetary Actions. Manager messages, future quest support, monitoring, follow- up calls, and ticket escalation are allowed when they are consistent with the evidence and do not imply unverified compensation.
